note_data.title: Software
keyPoints: **System software** manages the computer hardware and provides a platform for other software to run
**Application software** performs specific tasks for the user such as word processing or gaming
**Operating system** manages hardware resources, provides user interface, and controls file management
**Interrupt** is a signal sent to the processor to request immediate attention from the operating system
**High-level programming languages** use English-like commands and are easier for humans to understand
**Low-level programming languages** are closer to machine code and include assembly language
**Compiler** translates the entire high-level program into machine code before execution
**Interpreter** translates and executes high-level program code line by line during runtime
**Integrated Development Environment (IDE)** provides tools for writing, testing and debugging programs in one package

definitions.definition: System software that manages computer hardware resources and provides services for application programs
A signal sent to the processor requesting immediate attention, causing the processor to suspend current activities
A translator that converts the entire high-level language program into machine code before execution
A translator that converts and executes high-level language program code one line at a time
Integrated Development Environment - software that provides comprehensive tools for software development including editor, compiler and debugger

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Give characteristics or features with some detail
Show similarities AND differences between two items

modelAnswer.question: Explain the difference between a compiler and an interpreter. [4 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: A compiler translates the entire high-level language program into machine code before execution, whereas an interpreter translates and executes the program line by line during runtime. A compiler produces an executable file that can run independently, while an interpreter requires the original source code to be present each time the program runs. Programs compiled by a compiler run faster because translation has already occurred, but interpreter-based programs are easier to debug because errors are identified line by line during execution.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Compiler translates entire program before execution
Mark 2: Interpreter translates line by line during runtime
Mark 3: Compiler produces executable file/interpreter needs source code
Mark 4: Speed difference or debugging difference explained

commonMistakes: Never say 'compiler is faster' — always say 'compiled programs run faster because translation has already occurred'
Students often confuse system software with hardware — system software is still software, not physical components
Don't say 'interrupt stops the processor' — say 'interrupt causes processor to suspend current activities temporarily'
Avoid saying 'high-level languages are better' — explain they are 'easier for humans to understand' instead
Never write 'IDE compiles programs' — IDE contains a compiler as one of its tools
examinerTips: Software questions appear in every Paper 2 exam, usually worth 8-12 marks total
Always distinguish between 'during execution' for interpreter and 'before execution' for compiler
When comparing translators, mention both speed of execution AND ease of debugging
For operating system questions, focus on 'manages resources' rather than listing features
